content.problemStatement:
For a string SSS let the unique set of characters that occur in it one or more times be CCC. Consider a permutation of the elements of CCC as (c1,c2,c3...)(c_1, c_2, c_3 ... )(c1‚Äã,c2‚Äã,c3‚Äã...). Let f(c)f(c)f(c) be the number of times ccc occurs in SSS.

If any such permutation of the elements of CCC satisfies f(ci)=f(ci‚àí1)+f(ci‚àí2)f(c_i) = f(c_{i-1}) + f(c_{i-2})f(ci‚Äã)=f(ci‚àí1‚Äã)+f(ci‚àí2‚Äã) for all i‚â•3i \ge 3i‚â•3, the string is said to be a **dynamic string**.

Mr Bancroft is given the task to check if the string is dynamic, but he is busy playing with sandpaper. Would you help him in such a state?

Note that if the number of distinct characters in the string is less than 3, i.e. if ‚à£C‚à£<3|C| < 3‚à£C‚à£<3, then the string is always dynamic.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, a string SSS.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line "**Dynamic**" if the given string is dynamic, otherwise print "**Not**". (Note that the judge is case sensitive)

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§101 \leq T \leq 101‚â§T‚â§10
1‚â§‚à£S‚à£‚â§1051 \leq |S| \leq 10^51‚â§‚à£S‚à£‚â§105
SSS contains only lower case alphabets: aaa, bbb, ..., zzz

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testase 1: For the given string, C={a,b,c}C = \{a, b, c\}C={a,b,c} and f(a)=4,f(b)=1,f(c)=3f(a)=4, f(b)=1, f(c)=3f(a)=4,f(b)=1,f(c)=3. f(a)=f(c)+f(b)f(a) = f(c) + f(b)f(a)=f(c)+f(b) so the permutation (b,c,a)(b, c, a)(b,c,a) satisfies the requirement.
Testcase 2: Here too C={a,b,c}C = \{a, b, c\}C={a,b,c} but no permutation satisfies the requirement of a dynamic string.
Testcase 3: Here C={m,n,o,p}C = \{m, n, o, p\}C={m,n,o,p} and (m,n,o,p)(m, n, o, p)(m,n,o,p) is a permutation that makes it a dynamic string.
